question: Did the model generate exactly two images as strictly instructed in the prompt?
Does the text explicitly name the dessert 'Autumn Falling Leaves' as requested?
Does the first generated image depict a top-down view of the dessert placed specifically on a 'black matte slate plate'?
Does the first image clearly feature 'caramel maple leaves' and a 'sprinkle of gold leaf flakes' on the side?
Does the second generated image display a side cross-section view showing distinct internal layers (mousse, jelly, and biscuit base)?
Do the images exhibit high-quality, distinct rendering of material textures, specifically differentiating between the matte slate, glossy caramel, and metallic gold?
Does the text explicitly describe the ingredients and textural sensations (e.g., creamy, crunchy) for each layer of the dessert?
Is the tone of the text sophisticated and professional, suitable for a proposal to a three-Michelin-star restaurant?
Do the visual layers shown in the second image (mousse, jelly, biscuit) correspond semantically to the ingredients described in the adjacent text?
Is the dessert shown in the cross-section view (Image 2) visually consistent in shape, color, and style with the exterior view (Image 1)?
Are the images placed in a logical sequence, with the top-down view (Image 1) introducing the design and the cross-section (Image 2) accompanying the detailed layer breakdown?
Does the cross-section view demonstrate logical physical structure (e.g., biscuit base at the bottom, layers contained within the mousse geometry) rather than defying gravity or geometry?
Is the proposal practical and detailed enough (both visually and textually) to serve as a plausible design brief for a professional pastry chef?
